{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Kofun in Japan |name = Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun |image = [[File:Tsurugashima Tsurugaokainarijinjya Tumulus 1.JPG|250px]] |image_caption = Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun (March 2012) |location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tsurugashima|de|Tsurugashima (German)|ja|鶴ヶ島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Matsugaoka 3-chome |coordinates = {{ill|Template:Coord|de|Template:Coord (German)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (35|55|28.7|N|139|25|08.2|E|scale:10000) |shape = {{ill|Square Kofun|de|Square Kofun (German)|ja|方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |size = East–West 40 m, North–South 53 m |burial_facility = Multi-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|de|horizontal stone chamber (German)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |construction_period = Mid–late 7th century |occupant = |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Haji ware|de|Haji ware (German)|ja|土師器|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, iron fragments |designated_cultural_property = |special_notes = }} '''Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun''' (鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳) was formerly a {{ill|Square Kofun|de|Square Kofun (German)|ja|方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in what is now {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tsurugashima|de|Tsurugashima (German)|ja|鶴ヶ島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Kofun excavation history == When the {{ill|Japan Housing Corporation|de|Japan Housing Corporation (German)|ja|日本住宅公団|qq=draft|qq=draft}} planned a housing development in the area spanning {{ill|Kawagoe|de|Kawagoe (German)|ja|川越市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Tsurugashima, archaeological investigations began in [[1971年]] (Shōwa 46). By the {{ill|excavation survey|de|excavation survey (German)|ja|発掘調査|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[1973年]] (Shōwa 48), it became clear that several {{ill|Kofun|de|Kofun (German)|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including what was then called Tsurugaoka Tomb No.1, had existed. Researchers inferred that there had once been a group of kofun in the area and named it the “Tsurugaoka Kofun Group.” However, many of these tombs are thought to have been destroyed when the former {{ill|mountain forests|de|mountain forests (German)|ja|山林|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were converted into large-scale {{ill|fields|de|fields (German)|ja|畑地|qq=draft|qq=draft}} after World War II. The Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun was the only one in the Tsurugaoka Kofun Group that still retained a burial mound, but after {{ill|excavation survey|de|excavation survey (German)|ja|発掘調査|qq=draft|qq=draft}} carried out from [[1983年]] (Shōwa 58) to [[1984年]] (Shōwa 59), it was destroyed to allow housing development. Today, about 50 meters southwest from the tomb’s original location, the mound, the {{ill|stone chamber|de|stone chamber (German)|ja|石室|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|encircling moat|de|encircling moat (German)|ja|周溝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been reconstructed in a nearby park. == Location of the kofun == The Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun was situated on level ground at the southern edge of the Iruma Plateau, on the north bank of the {{ill|Arakawa (Kantō)|de|Arakawa (Kantō) (German)|ja|荒川 (関東)|lt=Arakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft}} water system’s {{ill|Obake River|de|Obake River (German)|ja|小畔川|lt=Kobaze River|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From its shape and other features, it is considered an {{ill|end-stage kofun|de|end-stage kofun (German)|ja|終末期古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built around the mid to late [[7世紀]] period. On the south bank of the Iruma River stands the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun|de|Sannōzuka Kofun (German)|ja|山王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is also regarded as an end-stage kofun and possibly an {{ill|Two-tiered round mound|de|Two-tiered round mound (German)|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Other kofun groups have been confirmed in the surrounding area of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun, but many have been lost due to development. == Mound and encircling moat == Although Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun barely retained its mound, it had been turned into a {{ill|tea field|de|tea field (German)|ja|茶畑|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and lost much of its original shape by the time of the excavation. Investigations revealed a row of stones thought to have once covered the base of the mound as {{ill|fukiishi|de|fukiishi (German)|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, indicating that the mound was roughly 20.5 meters east–west by 21 meters north–south. A trench-like {{ill|archaeological feature|de|archaeological feature (German)|ja|遺構|1=Feature_(archaeology)|lt=Archaeological Feature|qq=draft|qq=draft}} encircled the mound in segments. This trench formed a slightly tilted {{ill|square|de|square (German)|ja|四角形|lt=fang-shaped|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shape (with the north–south edges angled somewhat west). Although its exact depth and width varied, it is regarded as the {{ill|shūkō|de|shūkō (German)|ja|周溝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or encircling moat. Inside this moat, the area measured about 40 meters east–west and 53 meters north–south. Thus, the Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun is considered to have been a square kofun about 40 meters (E–W) by 53 meters (N–S), featuring a nearly 20.5 m by 21 m mound encircled by a flattened, base-like facility, then encompassed by the interrupted moat. No {{ill|haniwa|de|haniwa (German)|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were found at Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun. This also suggests that it was an end-stage kofun. == Main burial chamber == [[File:Tsurugashima Tsurugaokainarijinjya Tumulus Stone Chamber 1.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Reconstructed stone chamber (March 2012)]] The main burial chamber of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|de|horizontal stone chamber (German)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a multi-chamber layout shaped like a paddle, widest at the rear (so-called “羽子板” shape). The total length is 4.53 meters, of which the main burial chamber (玄室) is 2.58 meters long at its interior, with a maximum width of 2.40 meters. Paddle-shaped multi-chamber “straight-bodied” stone chambers widest at the rear are characteristic of end-stage kofun found around the Iruma Plateau in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Inside the main chamber, fist-sized river pebbles had been laid out across the floor, and a coffin base of green schist measuring 2.14 meters in length and 0.43 meters at its widest point was placed in the center. A green schist threshold stone was installed between the main chamber ({{ill|gen-shitsu|de|gen-shitsu (German)|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and the antechamber. The antechamber also had a floor of laid river pebbles. Though the passage ({{ill|engendō|de|engendō (German)|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and the forecourt area have been heavily damaged, there is evidence suggesting that another green schist threshold once separated the antechamber from the passage, and that the forecourt area opened outward in a ‘V’ shape. Before constructing the stone chamber itself, the builders dug an area roughly 6.60 meters east–west by 7.90 meters north–south, about 80–90 cm deep, and then used {{ill|rammed earth|de|rammed earth (German)|ja|版築|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to compact the ground in a foundation process known as “horikomi jigyō.” This method of foundation work is found in other prominent end-stage kofun in the Kantō region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun|de|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun (German)|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|de|Fuchū, Tokyo (German)|ja|府中市 (東京都)|lt=Fuchū City|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Tenmondai Kōnai Kofun|de|Tenmondai Kōnai Kofun (German)|ja|天文台構内古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mitaka|de|Mitaka (German)|ja|三鷹市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|Ana-Hachiman Kofun|de|Ana-Hachiman Kofun (German)|ja|穴八幡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ogawa, Saitama|de|Ogawa, Saitama (German)|ja|小川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Excavated artifacts == By the time of investigation, the tomb had already been plundered. Artifacts found within the stone chamber included about ten iron fragments thought to be nails, and from the shūkō (encircling moat) came {{ill|Haji ware|de|Haji ware (German)|ja|土師器|qq=draft|qq=draft}} pottery fragments. The styles of this Haji ware also point to a mid-to-late 7th-century construction date for the tomb. == Notable features == Among the unique features of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun are its square mound, the flattened, base-like structure surrounding it, and the discontinuous trench encircling its outer perimeter—an unusual design seldom seen elsewhere“Kofun of Musashi and Sagami” (2007), p.27. The paddle-shaped, multi-chamber “straight-bodied” stone chamber that widens toward the rear is especially characteristic of end-stage kofun in the Iruma Plateau region of Saitama Prefecture. From the mid to late 7th century, there is a trend in {{ill|Musashi Province|de|Musashi Province (German)|ja|武蔵国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} where particular stone chamber forms appear collectively in certain localities. It has been suggested that this reflects emerging local group identities around the time when the ancient {{ill|District (Japan)|de|District (Japan) (German)|ja|評|qq=draft|qq=draft}} system was introduced‘Saitama Kōko’ 43 (2008), p.41. At about 40 meters (E–W) by 53 meters (N–S), this square kofun is relatively large for an end-stage tomb and is considered the burial site of a powerful local ruler of Musashi Province in that era. == Reconstructed kofun == About 50 meters southeast of the Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun’s original location, inside Sakai Park, a reconstructed version of the tomb has been built. The stone chamber and the tomb height have been reproduced in their original dimensions as revealed by the excavation, while the diameter of the burial mound and the surrounding moat have been reproduced at half scale. == Footnotes == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== References == * Saitama Prefecture Excavation Report No.8, Japan Housing Corporation (Kawagoe, Tsurugashima Area) Archaeological Excavation Report, Saitama Prefectural Board of Education, 1976. * Saitama Prefecture Excavation Report No.45, Housing and Urban Development Corporation (Tsurugaoka E-Block) Archaeological Excavation Report, Saitama Archaeological Research Foundation, 1985. * Fuchū City Board of Education / Fuchū City Archaeological Research Society (eds.), “Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun: Preliminary Report,” Gakuseisha, 2005. * “Kofun of Musashi and Sagami,” in <季刊考古学 Quarterly Archaeology> Extra Issue 15, Yūzankaku, 2007. * ‘Saitama Kōko’ 43, Saitama Archaeological Society, 2008. == See Also == * {{ill|Square Kofun|de|Square Kofun (German)|ja|方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|de|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun''' (鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳)--> {{translated page|ja|鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳|version=56643696|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tsurugashima]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳]] [[ja:稲荷山公園古墳群]] [[ja:稲荷台古墳群]] [[ja:祇園・長須賀古墳群]] [[ja:稲荷前古墳群]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[ja:%E3%80%8C%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E3%80%8D%E3%81%AE%E4%BB%98%E3%81%8F%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E5%88%97%E5%B3%B6%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E4%B8%80%E8%A6%A7]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari ]] [[de:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Kofun in Japan |name = Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun |image = [[File:Tsurugashima Tsurugaokainarijinjya Tumulus 1.JPG|250px]] |image_caption = Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun (March 2012) |location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tsurugashima|de|Tsurugashima (German)|ja|鶴ヶ島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Matsugaoka 3-chome |coordinates = {{ill|Template:Coord|de|Template:Coord (German)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (35|55|28.7|N|139|25|08.2|E|scale:10000) |shape = {{ill|Square Kofun|de|Square Kofun (German)|ja|方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |size = East–West 40 m, North–South 53 m |burial_facility = Multi-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|de|horizontal stone chamber (German)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |construction_period = Mid–late 7th century |occupant = |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Haji ware|de|Haji ware (German)|ja|土師器|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, iron fragments |designated_cultural_property = |special_notes = }} '''Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun''' (鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳) was formerly a {{ill|Square Kofun|de|Square Kofun (German)|ja|方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in what is now {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tsurugashima|de|Tsurugashima (German)|ja|鶴ヶ島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Kofun excavation history == When the {{ill|Japan Housing Corporation|de|Japan Housing Corporation (German)|ja|日本住宅公団|qq=draft|qq=draft}} planned a housing development in the area spanning {{ill|Kawagoe|de|Kawagoe (German)|ja|川越市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Tsurugashima, archaeological investigations began in [[1971年]] (Shōwa 46). By the {{ill|excavation survey|de|excavation survey (German)|ja|発掘調査|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[1973年]] (Shōwa 48), it became clear that several {{ill|Kofun|de|Kofun (German)|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including what was then called Tsurugaoka Tomb No.1, had existed. Researchers inferred that there had once been a group of kofun in the area and named it the “Tsurugaoka Kofun Group.” However, many of these tombs are thought to have been destroyed when the former {{ill|mountain forests|de|mountain forests (German)|ja|山林|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were converted into large-scale {{ill|fields|de|fields (German)|ja|畑地|qq=draft|qq=draft}} after World War II. The Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun was the only one in the Tsurugaoka Kofun Group that still retained a burial mound, but after {{ill|excavation survey|de|excavation survey (German)|ja|発掘調査|qq=draft|qq=draft}} carried out from [[1983年]] (Shōwa 58) to [[1984年]] (Shōwa 59), it was destroyed to allow housing development. Today, about 50 meters southwest from the tomb’s original location, the mound, the {{ill|stone chamber|de|stone chamber (German)|ja|石室|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|encircling moat|de|encircling moat (German)|ja|周溝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been reconstructed in a nearby park. == Location of the kofun == The Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun was situated on level ground at the southern edge of the Iruma Plateau, on the north bank of the {{ill|Arakawa (Kantō)|de|Arakawa (Kantō) (German)|ja|荒川 (関東)|lt=Arakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft}} water system’s {{ill|Obake River|de|Obake River (German)|ja|小畔川|lt=Kobaze River|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From its shape and other features, it is considered an {{ill|end-stage kofun|de|end-stage kofun (German)|ja|終末期古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} built around the mid to late [[7世紀]] period. On the south bank of the Iruma River stands the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun|de|Sannōzuka Kofun (German)|ja|山王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is also regarded as an end-stage kofun and possibly an {{ill|Two-tiered round mound|de|Two-tiered round mound (German)|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Other kofun groups have been confirmed in the surrounding area of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun, but many have been lost due to development. == Mound and encircling moat == Although Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun barely retained its mound, it had been turned into a {{ill|tea field|de|tea field (German)|ja|茶畑|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and lost much of its original shape by the time of the excavation. Investigations revealed a row of stones thought to have once covered the base of the mound as {{ill|fukiishi|de|fukiishi (German)|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, indicating that the mound was roughly 20.5 meters east–west by 21 meters north–south. A trench-like {{ill|archaeological feature|de|archaeological feature (German)|ja|遺構|1=Feature_(archaeology)|lt=Archaeological Feature|qq=draft|qq=draft}} encircled the mound in segments. This trench formed a slightly tilted {{ill|square|de|square (German)|ja|四角形|lt=fang-shaped|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shape (with the north–south edges angled somewhat west). Although its exact depth and width varied, it is regarded as the {{ill|shūkō|de|shūkō (German)|ja|周溝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or encircling moat. Inside this moat, the area measured about 40 meters east–west and 53 meters north–south. Thus, the Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun is considered to have been a square kofun about 40 meters (E–W) by 53 meters (N–S), featuring a nearly 20.5 m by 21 m mound encircled by a flattened, base-like facility, then encompassed by the interrupted moat. No {{ill|haniwa|de|haniwa (German)|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were found at Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun. This also suggests that it was an end-stage kofun. == Main burial chamber == [[File:Tsurugashima Tsurugaokainarijinjya Tumulus Stone Chamber 1.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Reconstructed stone chamber (March 2012)]] The main burial chamber of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|de|horizontal stone chamber (German)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a multi-chamber layout shaped like a paddle, widest at the rear (so-called “羽子板” shape). The total length is 4.53 meters, of which the main burial chamber (玄室) is 2.58 meters long at its interior, with a maximum width of 2.40 meters. Paddle-shaped multi-chamber “straight-bodied” stone chambers widest at the rear are characteristic of end-stage kofun found around the Iruma Plateau in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Inside the main chamber, fist-sized river pebbles had been laid out across the floor, and a coffin base of green schist measuring 2.14 meters in length and 0.43 meters at its widest point was placed in the center. A green schist threshold stone was installed between the main chamber ({{ill|gen-shitsu|de|gen-shitsu (German)|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and the antechamber. The antechamber also had a floor of laid river pebbles. Though the passage ({{ill|engendō|de|engendō (German)|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and the forecourt area have been heavily damaged, there is evidence suggesting that another green schist threshold once separated the antechamber from the passage, and that the forecourt area opened outward in a ‘V’ shape. Before constructing the stone chamber itself, the builders dug an area roughly 6.60 meters east–west by 7.90 meters north–south, about 80–90 cm deep, and then used {{ill|rammed earth|de|rammed earth (German)|ja|版築|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to compact the ground in a foundation process known as “horikomi jigyō.” This method of foundation work is found in other prominent end-stage kofun in the Kantō region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun|de|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun (German)|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|de|Fuchū, Tokyo (German)|ja|府中市 (東京都)|lt=Fuchū City|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Tenmondai Kōnai Kofun|de|Tenmondai Kōnai Kofun (German)|ja|天文台構内古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mitaka|de|Mitaka (German)|ja|三鷹市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|Ana-Hachiman Kofun|de|Ana-Hachiman Kofun (German)|ja|穴八幡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ogawa, Saitama|de|Ogawa, Saitama (German)|ja|小川町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Excavated artifacts == By the time of investigation, the tomb had already been plundered. Artifacts found within the stone chamber included about ten iron fragments thought to be nails, and from the shūkō (encircling moat) came {{ill|Haji ware|de|Haji ware (German)|ja|土師器|qq=draft|qq=draft}} pottery fragments. The styles of this Haji ware also point to a mid-to-late 7th-century construction date for the tomb. == Notable features == Among the unique features of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun are its square mound, the flattened, base-like structure surrounding it, and the discontinuous trench encircling its outer perimeter—an unusual design seldom seen elsewhere“Kofun of Musashi and Sagami” (2007), p.27. The paddle-shaped, multi-chamber “straight-bodied” stone chamber that widens toward the rear is especially characteristic of end-stage kofun in the Iruma Plateau region of Saitama Prefecture. From the mid to late 7th century, there is a trend in {{ill|Musashi Province|de|Musashi Province (German)|ja|武蔵国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} where particular stone chamber forms appear collectively in certain localities. It has been suggested that this reflects emerging local group identities around the time when the ancient {{ill|District (Japan)|de|District (Japan) (German)|ja|評|qq=draft|qq=draft}} system was introduced‘Saitama Kōko’ 43 (2008), p.41. At about 40 meters (E–W) by 53 meters (N–S), this square kofun is relatively large for an end-stage tomb and is considered the burial site of a powerful local ruler of Musashi Province in that era. == Reconstructed kofun == About 50 meters southeast of the Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun’s original location, inside Sakai Park, a reconstructed version of the tomb has been built. The stone chamber and the tomb height have been reproduced in their original dimensions as revealed by the excavation, while the diameter of the burial mound and the surrounding moat have been reproduced at half scale. == Footnotes ==
== References == * Saitama Prefecture Excavation Report No.8, Japan Housing Corporation (Kawagoe, Tsurugashima Area) Archaeological Excavation Report, Saitama Prefectural Board of Education, 1976. * Saitama Prefecture Excavation Report No.45, Housing and Urban Development Corporation (Tsurugaoka E-Block) Archaeological Excavation Report, Saitama Archaeological Research Foundation, 1985. * Fuchū City Board of Education / Fuchū City Archaeological Research Society (eds.), “Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun: Preliminary Report,” Gakuseisha, 2005. * “Kofun of Musashi and Sagami,” in <季刊考古学 Quarterly Archaeology> Extra Issue 15, Yūzankaku, 2007. * ‘Saitama Kōko’ 43, Saitama Archaeological Society, 2008. == See Also == * {{ill|Square Kofun|de|Square Kofun (German)|ja|方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|de|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun''' (鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳)--> {{translated page|ja|鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳|version=56643696|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tsurugashima]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳]] [[ja:稲荷山公園古墳群]] [[ja:稲荷台古墳群]] [[ja:祇園・長須賀古墳群]] [[ja:稲荷前古墳群]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[ja:%E3%80%8C%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E3%80%8D%E3%81%AE%E4%BB%98%E3%81%8F%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E5%88%97%E5%B3%B6%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E4%B8%80%E8%A6%A7]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari ]] [[de:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]]

From the mid to late 7th century, there is a trend in {{ill|Musashi Province|de|Musashi Province (German)|ja|武蔵国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} where particular stone chamber forms appear collectively in certain localities. It has been suggested that this reflects emerging local group identities around the time when the ancient {{ill|District (Japan)|de|District (Japan) (German)|ja|評|qq=draft|qq=draft}} system was introduced‘Saitama Kōko’ 43 (2008), p.41. At about 40 meters (E–W) by 53 meters (N–S), this square kofun is relatively large for an end-stage tomb and is considered the burial site of a powerful local ruler of Musashi Province in that era. == Reconstructed kofun == About 50 meters southeast of the Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun’s original location, inside Sakai Park, a reconstructed version of the tomb has been built. The stone chamber and the tomb height have been reproduced in their original dimensions as revealed by the excavation, while the diameter of the burial mound and the surrounding moat have been reproduced at half scale. == Footnotes ==
== References == * Saitama Prefecture Excavation Report No.8, Japan Housing Corporation (Kawagoe, Tsurugashima Area) Archaeological Excavation Report, Saitama Prefectural Board of Education, 1976. * Saitama Prefecture Excavation Report No.45, Housing and Urban Development Corporation (Tsurugaoka E-Block) Archaeological Excavation Report, Saitama Archaeological Research Foundation, 1985. * Fuchū City Board of Education / Fuchū City Archaeological Research Society (eds.), “Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun: Preliminary Report,” Gakuseisha, 2005. * “Kofun of Musashi and Sagami,” in <季刊考古学 Quarterly Archaeology> Extra Issue 15, Yūzankaku, 2007. * ‘Saitama Kōko’ 43, Saitama Archaeological Society, 2008. == See Also == * {{ill|Square Kofun|de|Square Kofun (German)|ja|方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|de|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun''' (鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳)--> {{translated page|ja|鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳|version=56643696|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tsurugashima]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]][[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳]] [[ja:稲荷山公園古墳群]] [[ja:稲荷台古墳群]] [[ja:祇園・長須賀古墳群]] [[ja:稲荷前古墳群]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[ja:%E3%80%8C%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E3%80%8D%E3%81%AE%E4%BB%98%E3%81%8F%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E5%88%97%E5%B3%B6%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E4%B8%80%E8%A6%A7]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari ]] [[de:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Kofun in Japan |name = Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun |image = [[File:Tsurugashima Tsurugaokainarijinjya Tumulus 1.JPG|250px]] |image_caption = Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun (March 2012) |location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Tsurugashima|de|Tsurugashima (German)|ja|鶴ヶ島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugashima|qq=}} Matsugaoka 3-chome |coordinates = {{ill|Template:Coord|de|Template:Coord (German)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Template:Coord|qq=}} (35|55|28.7|N|139|25|08.2|E|scale:10000) |shape = {{ill|Square Kofun|de|Square Kofun (German)|ja|方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |size = East–West 40 m, North–South 53 m |burial_facility = Multi-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|de|horizontal stone chamber (German)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |construction_period = Mid–late 7th century |occupant = |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Haji ware|de|Haji ware (German)|ja|土師器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji ware|qq=}}, iron fragments |designated_cultural_property = |special_notes = }} '''Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun''' (鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳) was formerly a {{ill|Square Kofun|de|Square Kofun (German)|ja|方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located in what is now {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Tsurugashima|de|Tsurugashima (German)|ja|鶴ヶ島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugashima|qq=}}. == Kofun excavation history == When the {{ill|Japan Housing Corporation|de|Japan Housing Corporation (German)|ja|日本住宅公団|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Housing Corporation|qq=}} planned a housing development in the area spanning {{ill|Kawagoe|de|Kawagoe (German)|ja|川越市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawagoe|qq=}} and Tsurugashima, archaeological investigations began in [[1971年]] (Shōwa 46). By the {{ill|excavation survey|de|excavation survey (German)|ja|発掘調査|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/excavation survey|qq=}} in [[1973年]] (Shōwa 48), it became clear that several {{ill|Kofun|de|Kofun (German)|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}}, including what was then called Tsurugaoka Tomb No.1, had existed. Researchers inferred that there had once been a group of kofun in the area and named it the “Tsurugaoka Kofun Group.” However, many of these tombs are thought to have been destroyed when the former {{ill|mountain forests|de|mountain forests (German)|ja|山林|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mountain forests|qq=}} were converted into large-scale {{ill|fields|de|fields (German)|ja|畑地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fields|qq=}} after World War II. The Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun was the only one in the Tsurugaoka Kofun Group that still retained a burial mound, but after {{ill|excavation survey|de|excavation survey (German)|ja|発掘調査|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/excavation survey|qq=}} carried out from [[1983年]] (Shōwa 58) to [[1984年]] (Shōwa 59), it was destroyed to allow housing development. Today, about 50 meters southwest from the tomb’s original location, the mound, the {{ill|stone chamber|de|stone chamber (German)|ja|石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|qq=}}, and the {{ill|encircling moat|de|encircling moat (German)|ja|周溝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/encircling moat|qq=}} have been reconstructed in a nearby park. == Location of the kofun == The Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun was situated on level ground at the southern edge of the Iruma Plateau, on the north bank of the {{ill|Arakawa (Kantō)|de|Arakawa (Kantō) (German)|ja|荒川 (関東)|lt=Arakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa (Kantō)|qq=}} water system’s {{ill|Obake River|de|Obake River (German)|ja|小畔川|lt=Kobaze River|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obake River|qq=}}. From its shape and other features, it is considered an {{ill|end-stage kofun|de|end-stage kofun (German)|ja|終末期古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/end-stage kofun|qq=}} built around the mid to late [[7世紀]] period. On the south bank of the Iruma River stands the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun|de|Sannōzuka Kofun (German)|ja|山王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, which is also regarded as an end-stage kofun and possibly an {{ill|Two-tiered round mound|de|Two-tiered round mound (German)|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Two-tiered round mound|qq=}}. Other kofun groups have been confirmed in the surrounding area of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun, but many have been lost due to development. == Mound and encircling moat == Although Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun barely retained its mound, it had been turned into a {{ill|tea field|de|tea field (German)|ja|茶畑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tea field|qq=}} and lost much of its original shape by the time of the excavation. Investigations revealed a row of stones thought to have once covered the base of the mound as {{ill|fukiishi|de|fukiishi (German)|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}}, indicating that the mound was roughly 20.5 meters east–west by 21 meters north–south. A trench-like {{ill|archaeological feature|de|archaeological feature (German)|ja|遺構|1=Feature_(archaeology)|lt=Archaeological Feature|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeological feature|13=User:Immanuelle/Feature_(archaeology)|qq=}} encircled the mound in segments. This trench formed a slightly tilted {{ill|square|de|square (German)|ja|四角形|lt=fang-shaped|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square|qq=}} shape (with the north–south edges angled somewhat west). Although its exact depth and width varied, it is regarded as the {{ill|shūkō|de|shūkō (German)|ja|周溝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shūkō|qq=}}, or encircling moat. Inside this moat, the area measured about 40 meters east–west and 53 meters north–south. Thus, the Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun is considered to have been a square kofun about 40 meters (E–W) by 53 meters (N–S), featuring a nearly 20.5 m by 21 m mound encircled by a flattened, base-like facility, then encompassed by the interrupted moat. No {{ill|haniwa|de|haniwa (German)|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} were found at Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun. This also suggests that it was an end-stage kofun. == Main burial chamber == [[File:Tsurugashima Tsurugaokainarijinjya Tumulus Stone Chamber 1.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Reconstructed stone chamber (March 2012)]] The main burial chamber of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|de|horizontal stone chamber (German)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a multi-chamber layout shaped like a paddle, widest at the rear (so-called “羽子板” shape). The total length is 4.53 meters, of which the main burial chamber (玄室) is 2.58 meters long at its interior, with a maximum width of 2.40 meters. Paddle-shaped multi-chamber “straight-bodied” stone chambers widest at the rear are characteristic of end-stage kofun found around the Iruma Plateau in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}. Inside the main chamber, fist-sized river pebbles had been laid out across the floor, and a coffin base of green schist measuring 2.14 meters in length and 0.43 meters at its widest point was placed in the center. A green schist threshold stone was installed between the main chamber ({{ill|gen-shitsu|de|gen-shitsu (German)|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gen-shitsu|qq=}}) and the antechamber. The antechamber also had a floor of laid river pebbles. Though the passage ({{ill|engendō|de|engendō (German)|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/engendō|qq=}}) and the forecourt area have been heavily damaged, there is evidence suggesting that another green schist threshold once separated the antechamber from the passage, and that the forecourt area opened outward in a ‘V’ shape. Before constructing the stone chamber itself, the builders dug an area roughly 6.60 meters east–west by 7.90 meters north–south, about 80–90 cm deep, and then used {{ill|rammed earth|de|rammed earth (German)|ja|版築|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rammed earth|qq=}} to compact the ground in a foundation process known as “horikomi jigyō.” This method of foundation work is found in other prominent end-stage kofun in the Kantō region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun|de|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun (German)|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|de|Fuchū, Tokyo (German)|ja|府中市 (東京都)|lt=Fuchū City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tenmondai Kōnai Kofun|de|Tenmondai Kōnai Kofun (German)|ja|天文台構内古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kōnai Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Mitaka|de|Mitaka (German)|ja|三鷹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitaka|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Ana-Hachiman Kofun|de|Ana-Hachiman Kofun (German)|ja|穴八幡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ana-Hachiman Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Ogawa, Saitama|de|Ogawa, Saitama (German)|ja|小川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawa, Saitama|qq=}}. == Excavated artifacts == By the time of investigation, the tomb had already been plundered. Artifacts found within the stone chamber included about ten iron fragments thought to be nails, and from the shūkō (encircling moat) came {{ill|Haji ware|de|Haji ware (German)|ja|土師器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji ware|qq=}} pottery fragments. The styles of this Haji ware also point to a mid-to-late 7th-century construction date for the tomb. == Notable features == Among the unique features of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun are its square mound, the flattened, base-like structure surrounding it, and the discontinuous trench encircling its outer perimeter—an unusual design seldom seen elsewhere“Kofun of Musashi and Sagami” (2007), p.27. The paddle-shaped, multi-chamber “straight-bodied” stone chamber that widens toward the rear is especially characteristic of end-stage kofun in the Iruma Plateau region of Saitama Prefecture. From the mid to late 7th century, there is a trend in {{ill|Musashi Province|de|Musashi Province (German)|ja|武蔵国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province|qq=}} where particular stone chamber forms appear collectively in certain localities. It has been suggested that this reflects emerging local group identities around the time when the ancient {{ill|District (Japan)|de|District (Japan) (German)|ja|評|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District (Japan)|qq=}} system was introduced‘Saitama Kōko’ 43 (2008), p.41. At about 40 meters (E–W) by 53 meters (N–S), this square kofun is relatively large for an end-stage tomb and is considered the burial site of a powerful local ruler of Musashi Province in that era. == Reconstructed kofun == About 50 meters southeast of the Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun’s original location, inside Sakai Park, a reconstructed version of the tomb has been built. The stone chamber and the tomb height have been reproduced in their original dimensions as revealed by the excavation, while the diameter of the burial mound and the surrounding moat have been reproduced at half scale. == Footnotes ==
== References == * Saitama Prefecture Excavation Report No.8, Japan Housing Corporation (Kawagoe, Tsurugashima Area) Archaeological Excavation Report, Saitama Prefectural Board of Education, 1976. * Saitama Prefecture Excavation Report No.45, Housing and Urban Development Corporation (Tsurugaoka E-Block) Archaeological Excavation Report, Saitama Archaeological Research Foundation, 1985. * Fuchū City Board of Education / Fuchū City Archaeological Research Society (eds.), “Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun: Preliminary Report,” Gakuseisha, 2005. * “Kofun of Musashi and Sagami,” in <季刊考古学 Quarterly Archaeology> Extra Issue 15, Yūzankaku, 2007. * ‘Saitama Kōko’ 43, Saitama Archaeological Society, 2008. == See Also == * {{ill|Square Kofun|de|Square Kofun (German)|ja|方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|de|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} '''Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun''' (鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳)--> {{translated page|ja|鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳|version=56643696|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tsurugashima]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]][[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳]] [[ja:稲荷山公園古墳群]] [[ja:稲荷台古墳群]] [[ja:祇園・長須賀古墳群]] [[ja:稲荷前古墳群]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[ja:%E3%80%8C%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E3%80%8D%E3%81%AE%E4%BB%98%E3%81%8F%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E5%88%97%E5%B3%B6%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E4%B8%80%E8%A6%A7]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari ]] [[de:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]]

Researchers inferred that there had once been a group of kofun in the area and named it the “Tsurugaoka Kofun Group.” However, many of these tombs are thought to have been destroyed when the former {{ill|mountain forests|de|mountain forests (German)|ja|山林|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mountain forests|qq=}} were converted into large-scale {{ill|fields|de|fields (German)|ja|畑地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fields|qq=}} after World War II. The Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun was the only one in the Tsurugaoka Kofun Group that still retained a burial mound, but after {{ill|excavation survey|de|excavation survey (German)|ja|発掘調査|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/excavation survey|qq=}} carried out from [[1983年]] (Shōwa 58) to [[1984年]] (Shōwa 59), it was destroyed to allow housing development. Today, about 50 meters southwest from the tomb’s original location, the mound, the {{ill|stone chamber|de|stone chamber (German)|ja|石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|qq=}}, and the {{ill|encircling moat|de|encircling moat (German)|ja|周溝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/encircling moat|qq=}} have been reconstructed in a nearby park. == Location of the kofun == The Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun was situated on level ground at the southern edge of the Iruma Plateau, on the north bank of the {{ill|Arakawa (Kantō)|de|Arakawa (Kantō) (German)|ja|荒川 (関東)|lt=Arakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa (Kantō)|qq=}} water system’s {{ill|Obake River|de|Obake River (German)|ja|小畔川|lt=Kobaze River|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obake River|qq=}}. From its shape and other features, it is considered an {{ill|end-stage kofun|de|end-stage kofun (German)|ja|終末期古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/end-stage kofun|qq=}} built around the mid to late [[7世紀]] period. On the south bank of the Iruma River stands the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun|de|Sannōzuka Kofun (German)|ja|山王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, which is also regarded as an end-stage kofun and possibly an {{ill|Two-tiered round mound|de|Two-tiered round mound (German)|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Two-tiered round mound|qq=}}. Other kofun groups have been confirmed in the surrounding area of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun, but many have been lost due to development. == Mound and encircling moat == Although Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun barely retained its mound, it had been turned into a {{ill|tea field|de|tea field (German)|ja|茶畑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tea field|qq=}} and lost much of its original shape by the time of the excavation. Investigations revealed a row of stones thought to have once covered the base of the mound as {{ill|fukiishi|de|fukiishi (German)|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}}, indicating that the mound was roughly 20.5 meters east–west by 21 meters north–south. A trench-like {{ill|archaeological feature|de|archaeological feature (German)|ja|遺構|1=Feature_(archaeology)|lt=Archaeological Feature|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeological feature|13=User:Immanuelle/Feature_(archaeology)|qq=}} encircled the mound in segments. This trench formed a slightly tilted {{ill|square|de|square (German)|ja|四角形|lt=fang-shaped|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square|qq=}} shape (with the north–south edges angled somewhat west). Although its exact depth and width varied, it is regarded as the {{ill|shūkō|de|shūkō (German)|ja|周溝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shūkō|qq=}}, or encircling moat. Inside this moat, the area measured about 40 meters east–west and 53 meters north–south. Thus, the Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun is considered to have been a square kofun about 40 meters (E–W) by 53 meters (N–S), featuring a nearly 20.5 m by 21 m mound encircled by a flattened, base-like facility, then encompassed by the interrupted moat. No {{ill|haniwa|de|haniwa (German)|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} were found at Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun. This also suggests that it was an end-stage kofun. == Main burial chamber == [[File:Tsurugashima Tsurugaokainarijinjya Tumulus Stone Chamber 1.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Reconstructed stone chamber (March 2012)]] The main burial chamber of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|de|horizontal stone chamber (German)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a multi-chamber layout shaped like a paddle, widest at the rear (so-called “羽子板” shape). The total length is 4.53 meters, of which the main burial chamber (玄室) is 2.58 meters long at its interior, with a maximum width of 2.40 meters. Paddle-shaped multi-chamber “straight-bodied” stone chambers widest at the rear are characteristic of end-stage kofun found around the Iruma Plateau in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}. Inside the main chamber, fist-sized river pebbles had been laid out across the floor, and a coffin base of green schist measuring 2.14 meters in length and 0.43 meters at its widest point was placed in the center. A green schist threshold stone was installed between the main chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|lt=Genshitsu|lt=Gen-shitsu|de|gen-shitsu (German)|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gen-shitsu|qq=}}) and the antechamber. The antechamber also had a floor of laid river pebbles. Though the passage ({{ill|engendō|de|engendō (German)|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/engendō|qq=}}) and the forecourt area have been heavily damaged, there is evidence suggesting that another green schist threshold once separated the antechamber from the passage, and that the forecourt area opened outward in a ‘V’ shape. Before constructing the stone chamber itself, the builders dug an area roughly 6.60 meters east–west by 7.90 meters north–south, about 80–90 cm deep, and then used {{ill|rammed earth|de|rammed earth (German)|ja|版築|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rammed earth|qq=}} to compact the ground in a foundation process known as “horikomi jigyō.” This method of foundation work is found in other prominent end-stage kofun in the Kantō region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun|de|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun (German)|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|de|Fuchū, Tokyo (German)|ja|府中市 (東京都)|lt=Fuchū City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tenmondai Kōnai Kofun|de|Tenmondai Kōnai Kofun (German)|ja|天文台構内古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kōnai Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Mitaka|de|Mitaka (German)|ja|三鷹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitaka|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Ana-Hachiman Kofun|de|Ana-Hachiman Kofun (German)|ja|穴八幡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ana-Hachiman Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Ogawa, Saitama|de|Ogawa, Saitama (German)|ja|小川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawa, Saitama|qq=}}. == Excavated artifacts == By the time of investigation, the tomb had already been plundered. Artifacts found within the stone chamber included about ten iron fragments thought to be nails, and from the shūkō (encircling moat) came {{ill|Haji ware|de|Haji ware (German)|ja|土師器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji ware|qq=}} pottery fragments. The styles of this Haji ware also point to a mid-to-late 7th-century construction date for the tomb. == Notable features == Among the unique features of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun are its square mound, the flattened, base-like structure surrounding it, and the discontinuous trench encircling its outer perimeter—an unusual design seldom seen elsewhere“Kofun of Musashi and Sagami” (2007), p.27. The paddle-shaped, multi-chamber “straight-bodied” stone chamber that widens toward the rear is especially characteristic of end-stage kofun in the Iruma Plateau region of Saitama Prefecture. From the mid to late 7th century, there is a trend in {{ill|Musashi Province|de|Musashi Province (German)|ja|武蔵国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province|qq=}} where particular stone chamber forms appear collectively in certain localities. It has been suggested that this reflects emerging local group identities around the time when the ancient {{ill|District (Japan)|de|District (Japan) (German)|ja|評|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District (Japan)|qq=}} system was introduced‘Saitama Kōko’ 43 (2008), p.41. At about 40 meters (E–W) by 53 meters (N–S), this square kofun is relatively large for an end-stage tomb and is considered the burial site of a powerful local ruler of Musashi Province in that era. == Reconstructed kofun == About 50 meters southeast of the Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun’s original location, inside Sakai Park, a reconstructed version of the tomb has been built. The stone chamber and the tomb height have been reproduced in their original dimensions as revealed by the excavation, while the diameter of the burial mound and the surrounding moat have been reproduced at half scale. == Footnotes ==
== References == * Saitama Prefecture Excavation Report No.8, Japan Housing Corporation (Kawagoe, Tsurugashima Area) Archaeological Excavation Report, Saitama Prefectural Board of Education, 1976. * Saitama Prefecture Excavation Report No.45, Housing and Urban Development Corporation (Tsurugaoka E-Block) Archaeological Excavation Report, Saitama Archaeological Research Foundation, 1985. * Fuchū City Board of Education / Fuchū City Archaeological Research Society (eds.), “Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun: Preliminary Report,” Gakuseisha, 2005. * “Kofun of Musashi and Sagami,” in <季刊考古学 Quarterly Archaeology> Extra Issue 15, Yūzankaku, 2007. * ‘Saitama Kōko’ 43, Saitama Archaeological Society, 2008. == See Also == * {{ill|Square Kofun|de|Square Kofun (German)|ja|方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|de|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} '''Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun''' (鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳)--> {{translated page|ja|鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳|version=56643696|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tsurugashima]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]][[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳]] [[ja:稲荷山公園古墳群]] [[ja:稲荷台古墳群]] [[ja:祇園・長須賀古墳群]] [[ja:稲荷前古墳群]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[ja:%E3%80%8C%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E3%80%8D%E3%81%AE%E4%BB%98%E3%81%8F%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E5%88%97%E5%B3%B6%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E4%B8%80%E8%A6%A7]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari ]] [[de:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]]

[[File:Tsurugashima Tsurugaokainarijinjya Tumulus 1.JPG|250px]] |image_caption = Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun (March 2012) |location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Saitama Prefecture stone chamber (German)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone ware|qq=}}, iron fragments |designated_cultural_property = |special_notes = }} '''Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun''' (鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳) was formerly a {{ill|Square Kofun|de|Square Kofun (German)|ja|方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located in what is now {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Tsurugashima|de|Tsurugashima (German)|ja|鶴ヶ島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugashima|qq=}}. == Kofun excavation history == When the {{ill|Japan Housing Corporation|de|Japan Housing Corporation (German)|ja|日本住宅公団|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Housing Corporation|qq=}} planned a housing development in the area spanning {{ill|Kawagoe|de|Kawagoe (German)|ja|川越市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawagoe|qq=}} and Tsurugashima, archaeological investigations began in [[1971年]] (Shōwa 46). By the {{ill|excavation survey|de|excavation survey (German)|ja|発掘調査|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/excavation survey|qq=}} in [[1973年]] (Shōwa 48), it became clear that several {{ill|Kofun|de|Kofun (German)|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}}, including what was then called Tsurugaoka Tomb No.1, had existed. Researchers inferred that there had once been a group of kofun in the area and named it the “Tsurugaoka Kofun Group.” However, many of these tombs are thought to have been destroyed when the former {{ill|mountain forests|de|mountain forests (German)|ja|山林|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mountain forests|qq=}} were converted into large-scale {{ill|fields|de|fields (German)|ja|畑地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fields|qq=}} after World War II. The Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun was the only one in the Tsurugaoka Kofun Group that still retained a burial mound, but after {{ill|excavation survey|de|excavation survey (German)|ja|発掘調査|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/excavation survey|qq=}} carried out from [[1983年]] (Shōwa 58) to [[1984年]] (Shōwa 59), it was destroyed to allow housing development. Today, about 50 meters southwest from the tomb’s original location, the mound, the {{ill|Stone Chamber|lt=Stone Chamber|lt=Stone chamber|de|stone chamber (German)|ja|石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|qq=}}, and the {{ill|encircling moat|de|encircling moat (German)|ja|周溝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/encircling moat|qq=}} have been reconstructed in a nearby park. == Location of the kofun == The Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun was situated on level ground at the southern edge of the Iruma Plateau, on the north bank of the {{ill|Arakawa (Kantō)|de|Arakawa (Kantō) (German)|ja|荒川 (関東)|lt=Arakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa (Kantō)|qq=}} water system’s {{ill|Obake River|de|Obake River (German)|ja|小畔川|lt=Kobaze River|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obake River|qq=}}. From its shape and other features, it is considered an {{ill|end-stage kofun|de|end-stage kofun (German)|ja|終末期古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/end-stage kofun|qq=}} built around the mid to late [[7世紀]] period. On the south bank of the Iruma River stands the {{ill|Sannōzuka Kofun|de|Sannōzuka Kofun (German)|ja|山王塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, which is also regarded as an end-stage kofun and possibly an {{ill|Two-tiered round mound|de|Two-tiered round mound (German)|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Two-tiered round mound|qq=}}. Other kofun groups have been confirmed in the surrounding area of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun, but many have been lost due to development. == Mound and encircling moat == Although Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun barely retained its mound, it had been turned into a {{ill|tea field|de|tea field (German)|ja|茶畑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tea field|qq=}} and lost much of its original shape by the time of the excavation. Investigations revealed a row of stones thought to have once covered the base of the mound as {{ill|fukiishi|de|fukiishi (German)|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}}, indicating that the mound was roughly 20.5 meters east–west by 21 meters north–south. A trench-like {{ill|archaeological feature|de|archaeological feature (German)|ja|遺構|1=Feature_(archaeology)|lt=Archaeological Feature|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archaeological feature|13=User:Immanuelle/Feature_(archaeology)|qq=}} encircled the mound in segments. This trench formed a slightly tilted {{ill|square|de|square (German)|ja|四角形|lt=fang-shaped|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square|qq=}} shape (with the north–south edges angled somewhat west). Although its exact depth and width varied, it is regarded as the {{ill|shūkō|de|shūkō (German)|ja|周溝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shūkō|qq=}}, or encircling moat. Inside this moat, the area measured about 40 meters east–west and 53 meters north–south. Thus, the Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun is considered to have been a square kofun about 40 meters (E–W) by 53 meters (N–S), featuring a nearly 20.5 m by 21 m mound encircled by a flattened, base-like facility, then encompassed by the interrupted moat. No {{ill|haniwa|de|haniwa (German)|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} were found at Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun. This also suggests that it was an end-stage kofun. == Main burial chamber == [[File:Tsurugashima Tsurugaokainarijinjya Tumulus Stone Chamber 1.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Reconstructed stone chamber (March 2012)]] The main burial chamber of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|de|horizontal stone chamber (German)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a multi-chamber layout shaped like a paddle, widest at the rear (so-called “羽子板” shape). The total length is 4.53 meters, of which the main burial chamber (玄室) is 2.58 meters long at its interior, with a maximum width of 2.40 meters. Paddle-shaped multi-chamber “straight-bodied” stone chambers widest at the rear are characteristic of end-stage kofun found around the Iruma Plateau in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|de|Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}. Inside the main chamber, fist-sized river pebbles had been laid out across the floor, and a coffin base of green schist measuring 2.14 meters in length and 0.43 meters at its widest point was placed in the center. A green schist threshold stone was installed between the main chamber ({{ill|Genshitsu|lt=Genshitsu|lt=Gen-shitsu|de|gen-shitsu (German)|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gen-shitsu|qq=}}) and the antechamber. The antechamber also had a floor of laid river pebbles. Though the passage ({{ill|engendō|de|engendō (German)|ja|羨道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/engendō|qq=}}) and the forecourt area have been heavily damaged, there is evidence suggesting that another green schist threshold once separated the antechamber from the passage, and that the forecourt area opened outward in a ‘V’ shape. Before constructing the stone chamber itself, the builders dug an area roughly 6.60 meters east–west by 7.90 meters north–south, about 80–90 cm deep, and then used {{ill|rammed earth|de|rammed earth (German)|ja|版築|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rammed earth|qq=}} to compact the ground in a foundation process known as “horikomi jigyō.” This method of foundation work is found in other prominent end-stage kofun in the Kantō region, such as {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun|de|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun (German)|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Fuchū, Tokyo|de|Fuchū, Tokyo (German)|ja|府中市 (東京都)|lt=Fuchū City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuchū, Tokyo|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tenmondai Kōnai Kofun|de|Tenmondai Kōnai Kofun (German)|ja|天文台構内古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kōnai Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Mitaka|de|Mitaka (German)|ja|三鷹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitaka|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Ana-Hachiman Kofun|de|Ana-Hachiman Kofun (German)|ja|穴八幡古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ana-Hachiman Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Ogawa, Saitama|de|Ogawa, Saitama (German)|ja|小川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawa, Saitama|qq=}}. == Excavated artifacts == By the time of investigation, the tomb had already been plundered. Artifacts found within the stone chamber included about ten iron fragments thought to be nails, and from the shūkō (encircling moat) came {{ill|Haji ware|de|Haji ware (German)|ja|土師器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji ware|qq=}} pottery fragments. The styles of this Haji ware also point to a mid-to-late 7th-century construction date for the tomb. == Notable features == Among the unique features of Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun are its square mound, the flattened, base-like structure surrounding it, and the discontinuous trench encircling its outer perimeter—an unusual design seldom seen elsewhere“Kofun of Musashi and Sagami” (2007), p.27. The paddle-shaped, multi-chamber “straight-bodied” stone chamber that widens toward the rear is especially characteristic of end-stage kofun in the Iruma Plateau region of Saitama Prefecture. From the mid to late 7th century, there is a trend in {{ill|Musashi Province|de|Musashi Province (German)|ja|武蔵国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province|qq=}} where particular stone chamber forms appear collectively in certain localities. It has been suggested that this reflects emerging local group identities around the time when the ancient {{ill|District (Japan)|de|District (Japan) (German)|ja|評|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/District (Japan)|qq=}} system was introduced‘Saitama Kōko’ 43 (2008), p.41. At about 40 meters (E–W) by 53 meters (N–S), this square kofun is relatively large for an end-stage tomb and is considered the burial site of a powerful local ruler of Musashi Province in that era. == Reconstructed kofun == About 50 meters southeast of the Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun’s original location, inside Sakai Park, a reconstructed version of the tomb has been built. The stone chamber and the tomb height have been reproduced in their original dimensions as revealed by the excavation, while the diameter of the burial mound and the surrounding moat have been reproduced at half scale. == Footnotes ==
== References == * Saitama Prefecture Excavation Report No.8, Japan Housing Corporation (Kawagoe, Tsurugashima Area) Archaeological Excavation Report, Saitama Prefectural Board of Education, 1976. * Saitama Prefecture Excavation Report No.45, Housing and Urban Development Corporation (Tsurugaoka E-Block) Archaeological Excavation Report, Saitama Archaeological Research Foundation, 1985. * Fuchū City Board of Education / Fuchū City Archaeological Research Society (eds.), “Musashi Fuchū Kumano Shrine Kofun: Preliminary Report,” Gakuseisha, 2005. * “Kofun of Musashi and Sagami,” in <季刊考古学 Quarterly Archaeology> Extra Issue 15, Yūzankaku, 2007. * ‘Saitama Kōko’ 43, Saitama Archaeological Society, 2008. == See Also == * {{ill|Square Kofun|de|Square Kofun (German)|ja|方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|de|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture (German)|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} '''Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun''' (鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳)--> {{translated page|ja|鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳|version=56643696|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tsurugashima]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]][[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]] [[ja:鶴ヶ丘稲荷神社古墳]] [[ja:稲荷山公園古墳群]] [[ja:稲荷台古墳群]] [[ja:祇園・長須賀古墳群]] [[ja:稲荷前古墳群]] [[ja:元稲荷古墳]] [[ja:%E3%80%8C%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E3%80%8D%E3%81%AE%E4%BB%98%E3%81%8F%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E5%88%97%E5%B3%B6%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E4%B8%80%E8%A6%A7]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[sn:Tsurugaoka Inari ]] [[de:Tsurugaoka Inari Shrine Kofun]]
